# Swap.Htmx - LLM Reference Guide

> Repository: https://github.com/jdtoon/swap
> Documentation: https://github.com/jdtoon/swap/tree/main/lib/Swap.Htmx/docs

## WHAT IS SWAP.HTMX?

**Event-driven UI orchestration for ASP.NET Core + HTMX**

Swap.Htmx solves the "how do I update 15 partials from one button click?" problem. When events fire, distributed handlers respond—each updating one piece of the UI. All updates are batched into a single HTTP response via out-of-band (OOB) swaps.

**Key Benefits:**
- **8.5x smaller bundles** than React (22KB vs 877KB)
- **46% less code** for same features (650 vs 1,200 lines)
- **Event-driven architecture** (controllers fire events, handlers update UI)
- **Type-safe** (source generators eliminate magic strings)
- **Server-side state** (no client/server synchronization complexity)

**When to Use:**
- ✅ Dashboards, admin panels, analytics platforms
- ✅ CRUD applications, e-commerce sites
- ✅ Internal tools, reporting systems
- ✅ Any app where you'd consider React but want simpler DX

**When NOT to Use:**
- ❌ Offline-first PWAs
- ❌ Highly interactive UIs (Figma-like editors, real-time collaboration)
- ❌ Mobile apps (use React Native)

**The Mental Model:**
```
User Action → Controller fires event → Handlers update partials → One HTTP response → HTMX swaps DOM
```

No useState, no useEffect, no Redux. Just events and handlers.

---

## PHILOSOPHY & BEST PRACTICES

### The Distributed Handler Pattern (RECOMMENDED)

**Pattern:** Each handler updates ONE partial. Controllers don't know what updates.

```csharp
// ✅ GOOD: Controller fires event, doesn't know about UI
[HttpPost]
public IActionResult CompletePurchase(int productId)
{
    var purchase = _service.ProcessPurchase(productId);
    
    // Just fire the event with typed payload
    return SwapEvent(new PurchaseCompletedEvent 
    { 
        ProductId = productId,
        Amount = purchase.Amount,
        CustomerName = purchase.CustomerName
    }).Build();
}

// ✅ GOOD: Handlers are decoupled, each updates ONE thing
[SwapHandler]
public class RevenueHandler : ISwapEventHandler<PurchaseCompletedEvent>
{
    private readonly AnalyticsState _state;
    
    public Task HandleAsync(PurchaseCompletedEvent e, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        builder.AlsoUpdate("revenue-card", "_RevenueCard", _state);
        return Task.CompletedTask;
    }
}

[SwapHandler]
public class ProductStockHandler : ISwapEventHandler<PurchaseCompletedEvent>
{
    private readonly AnalyticsState _state;
    
    public Task HandleAsync(PurchaseCompletedEvent e, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        var product = _state.Products.First(p => p.Id == e.ProductId);
        builder.AlsoUpdate($"product-{e.ProductId}", "_ProductCard", product);
        return Task.CompletedTask;
    }
}
```

**Why This Works:**
- Adding new UI component = add handler (no controller changes)
- Handlers are testable in isolation
- Zero coupling between handlers
- Scales to 50+ partials without complexity growth

**Anti-Pattern:**
```csharp
// ❌ BAD: Controller knows about all UI targets
[HttpPost]
public IActionResult CompletePurchase(int productId)
{
    var purchase = _service.ProcessPurchase(productId);
    
    return SwapResponse()
        .AlsoUpdate("revenue-card", "_RevenueCard", GetRevenue())
        .AlsoUpdate($"product-{productId}", "_ProductCard", GetProduct(productId))
        .AlsoUpdate("category-electronics", "_CategoryCard", GetCategory("electronics"))
        .AlsoUpdate("region-west", "_RegionCard", GetRegion("West"))
        // ... 10 more updates
        .Build();
}
```

This tightly couples controller to UI structure. Use events instead.

### State Management: Server is Source of Truth

**Pattern:** Singleton state class, direct mutations, no synchronization.

```csharp
// ✅ GOOD: Simple C# class with business logic
public class AnalyticsState
{
    public decimal RevenueToday { get; set; }
    public int OrdersCount { get; set; }
    public List<Product> Products { get; } = new();
    
    // Computed properties (no React useMemo needed)
    public decimal AvgOrderValue => OrdersCount > 0 ? RevenueToday / OrdersCount : 0;
    
    // Business logic methods
    public void ProcessPurchase(int productId, decimal amount)
    {
        var product = Products.First(p => p.Id == productId);
        product.Stock--;
        product.Sales++;
        
        RevenueToday += amount;
        OrdersCount++;
    }
}

// Register as singleton
builder.Services.AddSingleton<AnalyticsState>();
```

**Why This Works:**
- State is ALWAYS current (server is source of truth)
- No client/server sync bugs
- Direct mutations (no immutability gymnastics)
- Computed properties are just C# getters

**Anti-Pattern:**
```csharp
// ❌ BAD: Trying to replicate React patterns in C#
public class AnalyticsState
{
    private decimal _revenue;
    public decimal Revenue 
    { 
        get => _revenue; 
        set 
        { 
            _revenue = value;
            OnPropertyChanged(nameof(Revenue));
            OnPropertyChanged(nameof(AvgOrderValue));
        } 
    }
    
    // Don't do this - you're not building a SPA
}
```

Server state doesn't need change tracking. Just mutate and render.

---

---

## INSTALLATION & QUICK START

### Installation

```bash
dotnet add package Swap.Htmx
```

### Quick Start with Template (RECOMMENDED for New Projects)

The **Modular Monolith template** provides production-ready architecture:

```bash
dotnet new install Swap.Templates
dotnet new swap-modular -n MyApp
cd MyApp/src
libman restore
dotnet run
```

**What you get:**
- Self-contained modules in `Modules/` folder
- SQLite + EF Core with migrations
- SPA-style navigation (HTMX-powered, no JS framework)
- Sample CRUD module showing all patterns
- Docker ready
- Integration tests

**For minimal starting point:** `dotnet new swap-mvc -n MyProject`

### Program.cs Setup

```csharp
var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);

builder.Services.AddControllersWithViews();  // or AddRazorPages()
builder.Services.AddSwapHtmx();  // Core services + handler auto-discovery

var app = builder.Build();

app.UseStaticFiles();
app.UseSwapHtmx();  // Middleware for request detection
app.MapControllers();

app.Run();
```

### Layout Requirements

```html
<head>
    <!-- Swap.Htmx CSS (toasts, validation styling) -->
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="~/_content/Swap.Htmx/css/swap.css" />
    
    <!-- HTMX core library -->
    <script src="https://unpkg.com/htmx.org@2.0.8"></script>
    
    <!-- Swap.Htmx client scripts (event handling, state sync) -->
    <script src="~/_content/Swap.Htmx/js/swap.client.js"></script>
</head>
```

### Enable Source Generators (for type-safe constants)

```xml
<!-- .csproj -->
<ItemGroup>
  <AdditionalFiles Include="Views\**\*.cshtml" />
  <AdditionalFiles Include="Modules\**\Views\**\*.cshtml" /> <!-- If using modules -->
</ItemGroup>
```

---

## GETTING STARTED CHECKLIST

Use this as a step-by-step guide when creating a new Swap.Htmx project or feature.

### ✅ Project Setup

1. **Install package:**
   ```bash
   dotnet add package Swap.Htmx
   ```

2. **Add services to `Program.cs`:**
   ```csharp
   builder.Services.AddSwapHtmx();
   ```

3. **Add middleware to `Program.cs`:**
   ```csharp
   app.UseSwapHtmx();
   ```

4. **Add scripts to `_Layout.cshtml` or `_Host.cshtml`:**
   ```html
   <link rel="stylesheet" href="~/_content/Swap.Htmx/css/swap.css" />
   <script src="https://unpkg.com/htmx.org@2.0.8"></script>
   <script src="~/_content/Swap.Htmx/js/swap.client.js"></script>
   ```

5. **Enable auto-layout suppression (RECOMMENDED):**
   ```csharp
   // Program.cs
   builder.Services.AddSwapHtmx(options =>
   {
       options.AutoSuppressLayout = true;
       options.DefaultNavigationTarget = "#main-content";
   });
   ```
   
   ```razor
   @* _ViewStart.cshtml *@
   @{
       Layout = Context.ShouldSuppressLayout() ? null : "_Layout";
   }
   ```

### ✅ Creating Your First Feature

1. **Create a view with HTMX attribute:**
   ```html
   <button hx-get="/Products/List" hx-target="#product-list">
       Load Products
   </button>
   <div id="product-list"></div>
   ```

2. **Create controller action:**
   ```csharp
   public class ProductsController : Controller
   {
       public IActionResult List()
       {
           var products = _service.GetAll();
           return this.SwapResponse()
               .WithView("_ProductList", products)
               .Build();
       }
   }
   ```

3. **Create partial view (`_ProductList.cshtml`):**
   ```html
   @model List<Product>
   
   @foreach (var product in Model)
   {
       <div>@product.Name - $@product.Price</div>
   }
   ```

### ✅ Adding Multi-Component Updates

1. **Define event class:**
   ```csharp
   public class ProductCreatedEvent
   {
       public int ProductId { get; set; }
       public string Name { get; set; } = "";
   }
   ```

2. **Create handlers with `[SwapHandler]`:**
   ```csharp
   [SwapHandler]
   public class ProductListHandler : ISwapEventHandler<ProductCreatedEvent>
   {
       private readonly IProductService _service;
       public ProductListHandler(IProductService service) => _service = service;
       
       public Task HandleAsync(ProductCreatedEvent e, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
       {
           var products = _service.GetAll();
           builder.AlsoUpdate("product-list", "_ProductList", products);
           return Task.CompletedTask;
       }
   }
   
   [SwapHandler]
   public class ProductCountHandler : ISwapEventHandler<ProductCreatedEvent>
   {
       private readonly IProductService _service;
       public ProductCountHandler(IProductService service) => _service = service;
       
       public Task HandleAsync(ProductCreatedEvent e, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
       {
           builder.AlsoUpdate("product-count", "_ProductCount", _service.Count());
           return Task.CompletedTask;
       }
   }
   ```

3. **Fire event from controller:**
   ```csharp
   [HttpPost]
   public IActionResult Create(ProductDto dto)
   {
       var product = _service.Create(dto);
       return this.SwapEvent(new ProductCreatedEvent 
       { 
           ProductId = product.Id, 
           Name = product.Name 
       })
       .WithCreatedToast("Product", product.Name)
       .Build();
   }
   ```

### ✅ Adding Forms with Validation

1. **Create form view:**
   ```html
   <form hx-post="/Products/Create" hx-target="#form-container">
       <div>
           <label asp-for="Name"></label>
           <input asp-for="Name" />
           <span asp-validation-for="Name" class="text-danger"></span>
       </div>
       
       <div>
           <label asp-for="Price"></label>
           <input asp-for="Price" type="number" step="0.01" />
           <span asp-validation-for="Price" class="text-danger"></span>
       </div>
       
       <button type="submit">Create</button>
   </form>
   ```

2. **Handle validation in controller:**
   ```csharp
   [HttpPost]
   public IActionResult Create(ProductDto dto)
   {
       if (!ModelState.IsValid)
       {
           return this.SwapValidationErrors(ModelState)
               .WithView("_CreateForm", dto)
               .Build();
       }
       
       var product = _service.Create(dto);
       return this.SwapResponse()
           .WithNavigation("/products")
           .WithCreatedToast("Product", product.Name)
           .Build();
   }
   ```

### ✅ Adding Server-Side State

1. **Create state class:**
   ```csharp
   public class ProductFilterState : SwapState
   {
       public string Search { get; set; } = "";
       public string Category { get; set; } = "all";
       public int Page { get; set; } = 1;
   }
   ```

2. **Add state to view:**
   ```html
   @model ProductViewModel
   
   <swap-state state="Model.State" />
   
   <input type="text" 
          name="search"
          value="@Model.State.Search"
          hx-get="/Products/Search"
          hx-target="#product-grid"
          hx-include="#product-filter-state"
          hx-trigger="keyup changed delay:300ms" />
   
   <div id="product-grid">
       <!-- Results -->
   </div>
   ```

3. **Bind state in controller:**
   ```csharp
   public IActionResult Search([FromSwapState] ProductFilterState state)
   {
       var products = _service.Search(state.Search, state.Category, state.Page);
       return this.SwapResponse()
           .WithView("_ProductGrid", products)
           .WithState(state)  // Syncs state back
           .Build();
   }
   ```

### ✅ Adding Type-Safe Constants (Optional but Recommended)

1. **Define event constants:**
   ```csharp
   [SwapEventSource]
   public partial class ProductEvents
   {
       public const string Created = "product.created";
       public const string Updated = "product.updated";
       public const string Deleted = "product.deleted";
   }
   ```

2. **Use generated properties:**
   ```csharp
   // Generated: ProductEvents.Product.Created, ProductEvents.Product.Updated, etc.
   return this.SwapEvent(ProductEvents.Product.Created, new { ... }).Build();
   ```

### ✅ Common Troubleshooting

**Issue:** Layout appears on HTMX requests (getting full page instead of partial)

**Solution:** Enable `AutoSuppressLayout` or add `_ViewStart.cshtml` with layout suppression:
```csharp
builder.Services.AddSwapHtmx(options => options.AutoSuppressLayout = true);
```

**Issue:** Validation errors not appearing

**Solution:** Ensure you're using `this.SwapValidationErrors(ModelState)`:
```csharp
if (!ModelState.IsValid)
    return this.SwapValidationErrors(ModelState).WithView("_Form", dto).Build();
```

**Issue:** Toast messages not showing

**Solution:** Ensure `swap.css` is loaded in layout:
```html
<link rel="stylesheet" href="~/_content/Swap.Htmx/css/swap.css" />
```

**Issue:** Handlers not firing

**Solution:** Ensure class has `[SwapHandler]` attribute and implements `ISwapEventHandler<T>`:
```csharp
[SwapHandler]  // Don't forget this!
public class MyHandler : ISwapEventHandler<MyEvent> { }
```

**Issue:** State not persisting between requests

**Solution:** Include state container with `hx-include`:
```html
<button hx-get="/Action" hx-include="#my-state">Click</button>
```

---

## THE CORE PATTERN: Events → Handlers → OOB Swaps

**This is the "aha!" moment for Swap.Htmx.**

### Example: Analytics Dashboard Purchase Flow

**1. User clicks "Purchase" button**
```html
<button hx-post="/Analytics/SimulatePurchase" 
        hx-swap="none"
        class="btn btn-primary">
    Purchase Product
</button>
```

**2. Controller fires event**
```csharp
[HttpPost]
public IActionResult SimulatePurchase()
{
    // Update state (source of truth)
    var productId = _state.Products[Random.Shared.Next(_state.Products.Count)].Id;
    _state.ProcessPurchase(productId, region: "West", customer: "John D.");
    
    // Fire event with typed payload
    return SwapEvent(AnalyticsEvents.Purchase.Completed, new PurchaseCompletedEvent
    {
        ProductId = productId,
        Region = "West",
        CustomerName = "John D."
    }).Build();
}
```

**3. Handlers respond (auto-discovered via [SwapHandler])**
```csharp
// Handler 1: Update revenue KPI
[SwapHandler]
public class RevenueHandler : ISwapEventHandler<PurchaseCompletedEvent>
{
    private readonly AnalyticsState _state;
    public RevenueHandler(AnalyticsState state) => _state = state;
    
    public Task HandleAsync(PurchaseCompletedEvent e, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        builder.AlsoUpdate("revenue-today", "_RevenueToday", _state);
        return Task.CompletedTask;
    }
}

// Handler 2: Update orders count
[SwapHandler]
public class OrdersHandler : ISwapEventHandler<PurchaseCompletedEvent>
{
    private readonly AnalyticsState _state;
    public OrdersHandler(AnalyticsState state) => _state = state;
    
    public Task HandleAsync(PurchaseCompletedEvent e, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        builder.AlsoUpdate("orders-count", "_OrdersCount", _state);
        return Task.CompletedTask;
    }
}

// Handler 3: Update specific product card
[SwapHandler]
public class ProductCardHandler : ISwapEventHandler<PurchaseCompletedEvent>
{
    private readonly AnalyticsState _state;
    public ProductCardHandler(AnalyticsState state) => _state = state;
    
    public Task HandleAsync(PurchaseCompletedEvent e, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        var product = _state.Products.First(p => p.Id == e.ProductId);
        builder.AlsoUpdate($"product-{e.ProductId}", "_ProductCard", product);
        return Task.CompletedTask;
    }
}

// ... 12 more handlers (each updating one partial)
```

**4. Server returns ONE HTTP response**
```html
<!-- Main response (can be toast or empty) -->
<div class="toast success">Purchase completed!</div>

<!-- OOB Swap 1 -->
<div id="revenue-today" hx-swap-oob="true">
    <div class="kpi-card">
        <h3>Revenue Today</h3>
        <p>$4,523.75</p>
    </div>
</div>

<!-- OOB Swap 2 -->
<div id="orders-count" hx-swap-oob="true">
    <div class="kpi-card">
        <h3>Orders</h3>
        <p>127</p>
    </div>
</div>

<!-- OOB Swap 3 -->
<div id="product-5" hx-swap-oob="true">
    <div class="product-card">
        <h4>Wireless Mouse</h4>
        <span class="stock text-danger">8 left</span>
    </div>
</div>

<!-- ... 12 more OOB swaps -->
```

**5. HTMX swaps each element**

HTMX finds every element with `hx-swap-oob="true"`, matches the ID, and swaps it into the DOM.

**Result:** One click → 15 partials update → One HTTP request → Zero JavaScript written.

**Compare to React:**
- React: Dispatch action → Reducer updates state → 60 components re-render (only 15 changed) → useMemo/React.memo optimization needed
- Swap.Htmx: Fire event → 15 handlers run → 15 surgical DOM updates → Done

---

## SWAPRESPONSEBUILDER: The Central API

All responses flow through `SwapResponseBuilder`. It accumulates updates and renders them in one response.

### Access Methods

```csharp
// In SwapController (inherits from Controller)
public class ProductsController : SwapController
{
    public IActionResult Index()
    {
        return SwapResponse()  // No "this." needed
            .WithView("_ProductList", products)
            .Build();
    }
}

// In standard Controller (composition)
public class ProductsController : Controller
{
    public IActionResult Index()
    {
        return this.SwapResponse()  // Extension method
            .WithView("_ProductList", products)
            .Build();
    }
}

// In Razor Pages
public class IndexModel : PageModel
{
    public IActionResult OnPost()
    {
        return this.SwapResponse()
            .WithView("_Partial", this)
            .Build();
    }
}

// In Minimal APIs
app.MapGet("/products", () =>
    SwapResults.Response()  // Static helper
        .WithView("_ProductList", products)
);
```

### Full API Reference

```csharp
SwapResponseBuilder
    // Main target (what hx-target receives)
    .WithView(string viewName, object? model = null)
    
    // Out-of-band swaps (update elements outside main target)
    .AlsoUpdate(string targetId, string viewName, object? model = null, SwapMode swapMode = SwapMode.OuterHTML)
    .AlsoUpdateMany<T>(IEnumerable<T> items, Func<T, string> idSelector, string viewName, SwapMode swapMode = SwapMode.OuterHTML)
    .AlsoUpdateIf(bool condition, string targetId, string viewName, object? model = null, SwapMode swapMode = SwapMode.OuterHTML)
    .AlsoUpdateIfExists(string targetId, string viewName, object? model = null, SwapMode swapMode = SwapMode.OuterHTML)
    
    // Navigation (SPA-style via HX-Location header)
    .WithNavigation(string path)
    .WithNavigation(string path, string? target, SwapMode? swap)
    .WithNavigation(NavigationOptions options)
    
    // Toasts (visual feedback)
    .WithToast(string message, ToastType type = ToastType.Info)
    .WithSuccessToast(string message)
    .WithErrorToast(string message)
    .WithWarningToast(string message)
    .WithInfoToast(string message)
    
    // CRUD toasts (contextual messages)
    .WithCreatedToast(string entityName, string? itemName = null)
    .WithUpdatedToast(string entityName, string? itemName = null)
    .WithDeletedToast(string entityName, string? itemName = null)
    .WithSavedToast(string entityName, string? itemName = null)
    .WithCrudToast(CrudOperation operation, string entityName, string? itemName = null)
    
    // Events (trigger client-side or fire server-side event chains)
    .WithTrigger(EventKey eventKey, object? payload = null)
    .WithTrigger(string eventName, object? payload = null)
    
    // State management (server-side state sync)
    .WithState(SwapState state, string? viewName = null)
    
    // Validation (model state errors)
    .WithValidationErrors(ModelStateDictionary modelState)
    
    // Redirects
    .WithRedirect(string url)
    
    // Server-Sent Events (broadcast to connected clients)
    .BroadcastSse(string eventName, object? payload = null)
    
    // Build final response
    .Build()  // Returns IActionResult
```

### SwapMode Options

Controls how OOB swaps are applied to target elements:

```csharp
public enum SwapMode
{
    OuterHTML,    // Replace entire element (default)
    InnerHTML,    // Replace inner content only
    BeforeBegin,  // Insert before element
    AfterBegin,   // Insert as first child
    BeforeEnd,    // Insert as last child (append)
    AfterEnd,     // Insert after element
    Delete,       // Remove the element
    None          // No swap
}
```

**Common uses:**
- `OuterHTML`: Replace entire card/component
- `InnerHTML`: Update content but keep wrapper (e.g., table tbody)
- `BeforeEnd`: Append to list (infinite scroll)
- `Delete`: Remove element (e.g., delete task row)

---
    .AlsoUpdate(string targetId, string viewName, object? model = null, SwapMode swapMode = SwapMode.OuterHTML)
    .AlsoUpdateMany<T>(IEnumerable<T> items, Func<T, string> idSelector, string viewName, SwapMode swapMode = SwapMode.OuterHTML)
    .AlsoUpdateIf(bool condition, string targetId, string viewName, object? model = null, SwapMode swapMode = SwapMode.OuterHTML)
    .AlsoUpdateIfExists(string targetId, string viewName, object? model = null, SwapMode swapMode = SwapMode.OuterHTML)
    
    // Navigation (SPA-style via HX-Location)
    .WithNavigation(string path)
    .WithNavigation(string path, string? target, SwapMode? swap)
    .WithNavigation(NavigationOptions options)
    
    // Toasts
    .WithToast(string message, ToastType type = ToastType.Info)
    .WithSuccessToast(string message)
    .WithErrorToast(string message)
    .WithWarningToast(string message)
    .WithInfoToast(string message)
    .WithCreatedToast(string entityName, string? itemName = null)
    .WithUpdatedToast(string entityName, string? itemName = null)
    .WithDeletedToast(string entityName, string? itemName = null)
    .WithSavedToast(string entityName, string? itemName = null)
    .WithCrudToast(CrudOperation operation, string entityName, string? itemName = null)
    
    // Triggers (client-side events)
    .WithTrigger(EventKey eventKey, object? payload = null)
    .WithTrigger(string eventName, object? payload = null)
    
    // State management
    .WithState(SwapState state, string? viewName = null)
    
    // Validation
    .WithValidationErrors(ModelStateDictionary modelState)
    
    // Navigation
    .WithRedirect(string url)
    
    // SSE broadcast
    .BroadcastSse(string eventName, object? payload = null)
    
    // Build
    .Build()  // Returns IActionResult
```

### SwapMode Enum

```csharp
public enum SwapMode
{
    OuterHTML,    // Replace entire element (default)
    InnerHTML,    // Replace inner content only
    BeforeBegin,  // Insert before element
    AfterBegin,   // Insert as first child
    BeforeEnd,    // Insert as last child (append)
    AfterEnd,     // Insert after element
    Delete,       // Remove the element
    None          // No swap
}
```

---

## COMMON PATTERNS & RECIPES

These are the most common scenarios you'll encounter. Use these as templates.

### Pattern 1: Search/Filter with Live Results

```html
<!-- View -->
<input type="text" 
       name="search"
       hx-get="/Products/Search"
       hx-target="#results"
       hx-trigger="keyup changed delay:300ms"
       placeholder="Search products...">

<div id="results">
    <!-- Results load here -->
</div>
```

```csharp
// Controller
public IActionResult Search(string search)
{
    var products = _service.Search(search);
    return this.SwapResponse()
        .WithView("_SearchResults", products)
        .Build();
}
```

### Pattern 2: Tabs with State Persistence

```html
<!-- View -->
<swap-state state="Model.State" />

<div class="tabs">
    <button hx-get="/Dashboard/Tab?tab=overview"
            hx-target="#tab-content"
            hx-include="#dashboard-state"
            class="@(Model.State.Tab == "overview" ? "active" : "")">
        Overview
    </button>
    <button hx-get="/Dashboard/Tab?tab=analytics"
            hx-target="#tab-content"
            hx-include="#dashboard-state"
            class="@(Model.State.Tab == "analytics" ? "active" : "")">
        Analytics
    </button>
</div>

<div id="tab-content">
    <!-- Tab content loads here -->
</div>
```

```csharp
// Controller
public IActionResult Tab([FromSwapState] DashboardState state, string tab)
{
    state.Tab = tab;
    var data = _service.GetTabData(tab);
    
    return this.SwapResponse()
        .WithView($"_Tab{tab}", data)
        .WithState(state)  // Syncs tab selection
        .Build();
}
```

### Pattern 3: Infinite Scroll

```html
<!-- View -->
<div id="products">
    @foreach (var product in Model.Products)
    {
        @await Html.PartialAsync("_ProductCard", product)
    }
</div>

@if (Model.HasMore)
{
    <div hx-get="/Products/LoadMore?page=@(Model.Page + 1)"
         hx-trigger="revealed"
         hx-swap="outerHTML"
         hx-target="this"
         class="load-more">
        Loading more...
    </div>
}
```

```csharp
// Controller
public IActionResult LoadMore(int page)
{
    var products = _service.GetPage(page, pageSize: 20);
    var hasMore = products.Count == 20;
    
    return this.SwapResponse()
        .WithView("_ProductCards", products)
        .AlsoUpdateIf(hasMore, "load-more-trigger", "_LoadMoreTrigger", new { Page = page })
        .Build();
}
```

### Pattern 4: Modal Dialog (CRUD)

```html
<!-- Button to open modal -->
<button hx-get="/Products/Edit/5"
        hx-target="#modal-container"
        hx-swap="innerHTML">
    Edit Product
</button>

<div id="modal-container">
    <!-- Modal loads here -->
</div>
```

```html
<!-- _EditModal.cshtml -->
<div class="modal-backdrop" onclick="this.remove()">
    <div class="modal" onclick="event.stopPropagation()">
        <form hx-post="/Products/Update/@Model.Id"
              hx-target="#modal-container"
              hx-swap="outerHTML">
            
            <input asp-for="Name" />
            <input asp-for="Price" />
            
            <button type="submit">Save</button>
            <button type="button" onclick="this.closest('.modal-backdrop').remove()">
                Cancel
            </button>
        </form>
    </div>
</div>
```

```csharp
// Controller
[HttpPost]
public IActionResult Update(int id, ProductDto dto)
{
    if (!ModelState.IsValid)
        return this.SwapValidationErrors(ModelState)
            .WithView("_EditModal", dto)
            .Build();
    
    _service.Update(id, dto);
    
    return this.SwapResponse()
        .WithView("_Empty", null)  // Close modal (replace with empty div)
        .AlsoUpdate($"product-{id}", "_ProductCard", _service.GetById(id))
        .WithUpdatedToast("Product", dto.Name)
        .Build();
}
```

### Pattern 5: Delete Confirmation

```html
<!-- Product card -->
<div id="product-@Model.Id">
    <h3>@Model.Name</h3>
    <button hx-delete="/Products/Delete/@Model.Id"
            hx-confirm="Delete this product?"
            hx-target="#product-@Model.Id"
            hx-swap="outerHTML swap:1s">
        Delete
    </button>
</div>
```

```csharp
// Controller
[HttpDelete]
public IActionResult Delete(int id)
{
    var product = _service.GetById(id);
    _service.Delete(id);
    
    return this.SwapResponse()
        .WithView("_Empty", null, SwapMode.Delete)  // Remove element
        .AlsoUpdate("product-count", "_ProductCount", _service.Count())
        .WithDeletedToast("Product", product.Name)
        .Build();
}
```

### Pattern 6: Dependent Dropdowns

```html
<!-- View -->
<select name="countryId" 
        hx-get="/Address/LoadStates"
        hx-target="#state-dropdown"
        hx-include="[name='countryId']">
    <option value="">Select Country</option>
    @foreach (var country in Model.Countries)
    {
        <option value="@country.Id">@country.Name</option>
    }
</select>

<div id="state-dropdown">
    <select name="stateId" disabled>
        <option>Select country first</option>
    </select>
</div>
```

```csharp
// Controller
public IActionResult LoadStates(int countryId)
{
    var states = _service.GetStates(countryId);
    return this.SwapResponse()
        .WithView("_StateDropdown", states)
        .Build();
}
```

### Pattern 7: Bulk Actions with Event Broadcasting

```html
<!-- View -->
<form id="bulk-form">
    @foreach (var task in Model.Tasks)
    {
        <div id="task-@task.Id">
            <input type="checkbox" name="taskIds" value="@task.Id" />
            <span>@task.Title</span>
        </div>
    }
    
    <button hx-post="/Tasks/BulkComplete"
            hx-include="#bulk-form"
            hx-swap="none">
        Complete Selected
    </button>
</form>
```

```csharp
// Controller
[HttpPost]
public IActionResult BulkComplete(int[] taskIds)
{
    foreach (var id in taskIds)
    {
        _service.Complete(id);
    }
    
    // Fire event - handlers will update relevant partials
    return this.SwapEvent(new TasksBulkCompletedEvent { TaskIds = taskIds })
        .WithSuccessToast($"Completed {taskIds.Length} tasks")
        .Build();
}

// Handler updates each task card
[SwapHandler]
public class BulkTaskCardHandler : ISwapEventHandler<TasksBulkCompletedEvent>
{
    public Task HandleAsync(TasksBulkCompletedEvent e, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        foreach (var taskId in e.TaskIds)
        {
            // Remove completed tasks from list
            builder.AlsoUpdate($"task-{taskId}", "", null, SwapMode.Delete);
        }
        return Task.CompletedTask;
    }
}
```

### Pattern 8: Optimistic UI Updates

```html
<!-- Button disables immediately, re-enables on response -->
<button hx-post="/Cart/AddItem/@Model.ProductId"
        hx-disabled-elt="this"
        hx-swap="none">
    Add to Cart
</button>

<div id="cart-count">@Model.CartCount</div>
```

```csharp
// Controller
[HttpPost]
public IActionResult AddItem(int productId)
{
    _cart.AddItem(productId);
    
    return this.SwapResponse()
        .AlsoUpdate("cart-count", "_CartCount", _cart.ItemCount)
        .WithSuccessToast("Added to cart")
        .Build();
}
```

### Pattern 9: Real-Time Dashboard (50+ Partials)

**See Analytics Module Demo for complete working example.**

```csharp
// Controller fires ONE event
[HttpPost]
public IActionResult SimulatePurchase()
{
    var productId = _state.Products[Random.Shared.Next(_state.Products.Count)].Id;
    _state.ProcessPurchase(productId, region: "West", customer: "John");
    
    return SwapEvent(AnalyticsEvents.Purchase.Completed, new PurchaseCompletedEvent
    {
        ProductId = productId,
        Region = "West"
    }).Build();
}

// 15 distributed handlers each update ONE partial
[SwapHandler]
public class RevenueHandler : ISwapEventHandler<PurchaseCompletedEvent>
{
    public Task HandleAsync(PurchaseCompletedEvent e, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        builder.AlsoUpdate("revenue-today", "_RevenueToday", _state);
        return Task.CompletedTask;
    }
}
// ... 14 more handlers (orders, products, regions, activity feed, etc.)
```

**Result:** One button click updates 15 components in 155ms via single HTTP request.

**Performance:** 50 partials = same 155ms response time. Linear scaling.

---

## SOURCE GENERATORS

Swap.Htmx provides three source generators to eliminate magic strings.

### 1. Event Source Generator

Generates type-safe event keys from string constants with dot notation.

```csharp
using Swap.Htmx.Attributes;

[SwapEventSource]
public partial class AppEvents
{
    public const string CartItemAdded = "cart.item.added";
    public const string CartCleared = "cart.cleared";
    public const string UserLoggedIn = "user.logged_in";
}

// Generated output:
// AppEvents.Cart.Item.Added  → "cart.item.added"
// AppEvents.Cart.Cleared     → "cart.cleared"
// AppEvents.User.LoggedIn    → "user.logged_in"
```

Usage:
```csharp
.WithTrigger(AppEvents.Cart.Item.Added, new { productId })
```

### 2. View Path Generator

Generates constants for .cshtml files in a folder.

```csharp
using Swap.Htmx.Attributes;

[SwapViewSource("Views/Products")]
public static partial class ProductViews { }

// Given files:
//   Views/Products/Index.cshtml
//   Views/Products/Edit.cshtml
//   Views/Products/_Grid.cshtml
//   Views/Products/_Row.cshtml

// Generated:
// ProductViews.Index         → "Index"
// ProductViews.Edit          → "Edit"
// ProductViews.Partials.Grid → "_Grid"
// ProductViews.Partials.Row  → "_Row"
```

### 3. Element ID Generator

Generates constants for element IDs found in .cshtml files.

```csharp
using Swap.Htmx.Attributes;

[SwapElementSource("Views/Products")]
public static partial class ProductElements { }

// Scans for id="..." attributes in .cshtml files
// Given: <div id="product-grid">, <div id="pagination">

// Generated:
// ProductElements.ProductGrid → "product-grid"
// ProductElements.Pagination  → "pagination"
```

### Combined Usage

```csharp
return this.SwapResponse()
    .WithView(ProductViews.Partials.Grid, products)
    .AlsoUpdate(ProductElements.Pagination, ProductViews.Partials.Pagination, paginationModel)
    .WithTrigger(AppEvents.Products.Listed)
    .Build();
```

---

## EVENT SYSTEM

### Option 1: EventKey (Manual)

```csharp
using Swap.Htmx.Events;

public static class CartEvents
{
    public static readonly EventKey ItemAdded = new("cart.itemAdded");
    public static readonly EventKey Cleared = new("cart.cleared");
}
```

### Option 2: SwapEventSource (Generated) — RECOMMENDED

```csharp
[SwapEventSource]
public partial class CartEvents
{
    public const string ItemAdded = "cart.item.added";
    public const string Cleared = "cart.cleared";
}
// Use: CartEvents.Cart.Item.Added
```

### Built-in SwapEvents Helper

```csharp
SwapEvents.Entity.Created("Product")   // "entity.product.created"
SwapEvents.Entity.Updated("Product")   // "entity.product.updated"
SwapEvents.Entity.Deleted("Product")   // "entity.product.deleted"
SwapEvents.UI.Refresh                  // "ui.refresh"
```

---

## EVENT CHAINS

Two approaches for automatic UI coordination when events fire.

### Approach 1: Distributed Handlers (RECOMMENDED)

Each handler updates one piece of the UI. Completely decoupled.

```csharp
// 1. Define event class
public class TaskCompletedEvent
{
    public int TaskId { get; set; }
    public string Title { get; set; } = "";
}

// 2. Create handlers (auto-discovered via [SwapHandler])
[SwapHandler]
public class TaskRowHandler : ISwapEventHandler<TaskCompletedEvent>
{
    public Task HandleAsync(TaskCompletedEvent e, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        builder.AlsoUpdate($"task-{e.TaskId}", "", null, SwapMode.Delete);
        return Task.CompletedTask;
    }
}

[SwapHandler]
public class StatsHandler : ISwapEventHandler<TaskCompletedEvent>
{
    private readonly IStatsService _stats;
    
    public StatsHandler(IStatsService stats) => _stats = stats;
    
    public async Task HandleAsync(TaskCompletedEvent e, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        var stats = await _stats.GetAsync();
        builder.AlsoUpdate("stats-panel", "_Stats", stats);
    }
}

// 3. Trigger from controller
[HttpPost]
public IActionResult Complete(int id)
{
    _service.Complete(id);
    return this.SwapEvent(new TaskCompletedEvent { TaskId = id }).Build();
}
```

### Approach 2: Centralized Configuration (ISwapEventConfiguration)

Define all reactions in one place.

```csharp
public class ProductEventConfig : ISwapEventConfiguration
{
    public void Configure(SwapEventBusOptions events)
    {
        events.When(ProductEvents.Created)
            .RefreshPartial("product-list", "_ProductList", ctx =>
            {
                var svc = ctx.RequestServices.GetRequiredService<IProductService>();
                return svc.GetAll();
            })
            .RefreshPartial("product-count", "_ProductCount", ctx =>
            {
                var svc = ctx.RequestServices.GetRequiredService<IProductService>();
                return new { Count = svc.Count() };
            })
            .SuccessToast("Product created!");
    }
}

// Register in Program.cs
builder.Services.AddSwapHtmx(options =>
{
    options.AddConfig<ProductEventConfig>();
});

// Trigger from controller
return SwapEvent(ProductEvents.Created);
```

### Event Chain Builder API

```csharp
events.When(eventKey)
    .RefreshPartial(targetId, viewName, modelFactory)
    .RefreshPartialAsync(targetId, viewName, asyncModelFactory)
    .SuccessToast(message)
    .ErrorToast(message)
    .WarningToast(message)
    .InfoToast(message)
    .AlsoTrigger(anotherEventKey)
    .Broadcast()  // SSE broadcast
    .Broadcast("room:roomName")  // SSE to specific room
```

---

## SWAPSTATE (Server-Side State)

Strongly-typed state management with automatic model binding.

### 1. Define State Class

```csharp
using Swap.Htmx.State;

public class InventoryState : SwapState
{
    public string Tab { get; set; } = "all";
    public int Page { get; set; } = 1;
    public int PageSize { get; set; } = 20;
    public string? Search { get; set; }
    public string SortBy { get; set; } = "name";
    public bool SortDesc { get; set; } = false;
}
```

### 2. Use in View

```html
@model InventoryViewModel

<!-- Renders hidden inputs in a container div -->
<swap-state state="Model.State" />

<!-- Reference with hx-include -->
<button hx-get="/Inventory/Grid"
        hx-target="#grid"
        hx-include="#inventory-state">
    Load
</button>
```

Renders as:
```html
<div id="inventory-state" data-swap-state style="display: none;">
    <input type="hidden" name="Tab" value="all" />
    <input type="hidden" name="Page" value="1" />
    ...
</div>
```

### 3. Bind in Controller

```csharp
public IActionResult Grid([FromSwapState] InventoryState state)
{
    var items = _service.Query(state.Tab, state.Page, state.Search);
    
    return this.SwapResponse()
        .WithView("_Grid", new GridViewModel { Items = items, State = state })
        .WithState(state)  // Auto-syncs state via OOB swap
        .Build();
}
```

### SwapState Base Class

```csharp
public abstract class SwapState
{
    // Auto-generated from class name (InventoryState → "inventory-state")
    public virtual string ContainerId { get; }
    
    // Change tracking
    public IReadOnlySet<string> ChangedProperties { get; }
    public bool HasChanges { get; }
    public void AcceptChanges();
    public IDisposable SuspendChangeTracking();
    
    // URL sync (for bookmarkable state)
    public virtual bool UrlSync => false;
    public virtual string UrlPrefix => "";
    
    // Serialization
    public Dictionary<string, string?> GetStateValues();
    public void SetStateValues(Dictionary<string, string?> values);
}
```

### Tag Helper Options

```html
<!-- Basic -->
<swap-state state="Model.State" />

<!-- Custom ID -->
<swap-state state="Model.State" id="my-state" />

<!-- With prefix for nested state -->
<swap-state state="Model.State" prefix="filter" />

<!-- Without container div -->
<swap-state state="Model.State" include-container="false" />
```

---

## NAVIGATION

SPA-style navigation that preserves toasts and triggers.

### The Problem

```csharp
// ❌ Redirect loses toast - response headers are discarded
return Redirect("/dashboard");
Response.HxTrigger("showToast", "Saved!");  // Never received!
```

### The Solution

```csharp
// ✅ WithNavigation uses HX-Location header
return this.SwapResponse()
    .WithNavigation("/dashboard")
    .WithSuccessToast("Saved!")
    .Build();
```

### Basic Usage

```csharp
// Simple navigation
.WithNavigation("/orders")

// Navigate to specific target
.WithNavigation("/inbox", target: "#main-content")

// Navigate with swap mode
.WithNavigation("/notifications", target: "#alerts", swap: SwapMode.BeforeEnd)
```

### Full Control

```csharp
.WithNavigation(new NavigationOptions
{
    Path = "/orders/123",
    Target = "#order-detail",
    Swap = SwapMode.OuterHTML,
    Select = "#order-content",     // CSS selector to pick from response
    Values = new { refresh = true }, // Additional values to send
    Headers = new { ["X-Custom"] = "value" }
})
```

### Common Patterns

```csharp
// Post-save redirect with toast
[HttpPost]
public IActionResult Create(OrderForm form)
{
    var order = _service.Create(form);
    
    return this.SwapResponse()
        .WithNavigation($"/orders/{order.Id}")
        .WithCreatedToast("Order", order.OrderNumber)
        .Build();
}

// Wizard step navigation
public IActionResult CompleteStep1(Step1Form form)
{
    _wizard.SaveStep1(form);
    
    return this.SwapResponse()
        .WithNavigation("/wizard/step2", target: "#wizard-content")
        .WithInfoToast("Step 1 complete!")
        .Build();
}

// Conditional navigation
[HttpPost]
public IActionResult Checkout()
{
    if (_cart.IsEmpty)
    {
        return this.SwapResponse()
            .WithNavigation("/products")
            .WithWarningToast("Your cart is empty")
            .Build();
    }
    
    return this.SwapResponse()
        .WithNavigation("/checkout/payment")
        .Build();
}
```

---

## `<swap-nav>` TAG HELPER

Simplified SPA-style navigation links without verbose HTMX attributes.

### The Problem

```html
<!-- Verbose and repetitive -->
<a href="/products" hx-get="/products" hx-target="#main-content" hx-push-url="true">Products</a>
<a href="/orders" hx-get="/orders" hx-target="#main-content" hx-push-url="true">Orders</a>
```

### The Solution

```html
<!-- Clean and declarative -->
<swap-nav to="/products">Products</swap-nav>
<swap-nav to="/orders">Orders</swap-nav>
```

### Configure Default Target

```csharp
// Program.cs
builder.Services.AddSwapHtmx(options =>
{
    options.DefaultNavigationTarget = "#main-content";
});
```

### Attributes

| Attribute | Description | Default |
|-----------|-------------|---------|
| `to` | Required. The URL to navigate to | — |
| `target` | CSS selector for target element | `SwapHtmxOptions.DefaultNavigationTarget` |
| `push-url` | Whether to update browser URL | `true` |

### Examples

```html
<!-- Basic navigation -->
<swap-nav to="/dashboard">Dashboard</swap-nav>

<!-- Override target -->
<swap-nav to="/sidebar" target="#sidebar-content">Refresh Sidebar</swap-nav>

<!-- Disable URL update -->
<swap-nav to="/modal/edit" push-url="false">Edit</swap-nav>

<!-- With CSS classes -->
<swap-nav to="/products" class="btn btn-primary">View Products</swap-nav>

<!-- Pass through hx-* attributes -->
<swap-nav to="/items" hx-swap="innerHTML" hx-indicator="#loading">Load Items</swap-nav>
```

### Renders As

```html
<swap-nav to="/products">Products</swap-nav>

<!-- Becomes -->
<a href="/products" hx-get="/products" hx-target="#main-content" hx-push-url="true">Products</a>
```

---

## AUTO-LAYOUT SUPPRESSION

Automatically return partials for HTMX requests, full pages for browser requests.

### The Problem

Without auto-suppression, you need `_ViewStart.cshtml` in every folder or manual layout checks:

```csharp
// Every HTMX action needs this
if (Request.IsHtmx())
    return PartialView("_Content", model);
return View(model);
```

### The Solution

```csharp
// Program.cs
builder.Services.AddSwapHtmx(options =>
{
    options.AutoSuppressLayout = true;
});
```

```razor
@* _ViewStart.cshtml (root level only) *@
@{
    Layout = Context.ShouldSuppressLayout() ? null : "_Layout";
}
```

### How It Works

| Request Type | Layout |
|--------------|--------|
| Browser navigation | `_Layout` applied |
| HTMX request (`HX-Request: true`) | Layout suppressed (partial only) |
| `<swap-nav>` clicks | Layout suppressed (partial only) |

### Benefits

- **One `_ViewStart.cshtml`** — No per-folder files needed
- **Same view, both contexts** — `return View(model)` works for HTMX and browser
- **Bookmarkable URLs** — Direct URL access renders full page with layout

---

## AUTO-SCAN SOURCE GENERATOR

Zero-configuration generation of view and element constants.

### Setup

```xml
<!-- .csproj -->
<ItemGroup>
  <AdditionalFiles Include="Views\**\*.cshtml" />
  <AdditionalFiles Include="Modules\**\Views\**\*.cshtml" />
</ItemGroup>
```

### Generated Classes

The generator automatically creates `SwapViews` and `SwapElements` classes:

```csharp
// Auto-generated from Views/Home/Index.cshtml, Views/Home/_Card.cshtml, etc.

// Use view paths
SwapViews.Home.Index         // "Index"
SwapViews.Home.Partials.Card // "_Card"
SwapViews.Shared.Layout      // "_Layout"

// Use element IDs (from id="..." in cshtml)
SwapElements.Home.Index.MainContent  // "main-content"
SwapElements.Home.Index.ProductGrid  // "product-grid"
```

### Folder Structure Mapping

```
Views/
├── Home/
│   ├── Index.cshtml          → SwapViews.Home.Index
│   ├── _Card.cshtml          → SwapViews.Home.Partials.Card
│   └── _List.cshtml          → SwapViews.Home.Partials.List
├── Products/
│   ├── Index.cshtml          → SwapViews.Products.Index
│   └── _Row.cshtml           → SwapViews.Products.Partials.Row
└── Shared/
    └── _Layout.cshtml        → SwapViews.Shared.Layout
```

### Modular Monolith Support

```
Modules/
├── Notes/
│   └── Views/
│       ├── Index.cshtml      → SwapViews.Notes.Index
│       └── _Form.cshtml      → SwapViews.Notes.Partials.Form
└── Tasks/
    └── Views/
        └── _Card.cshtml      → SwapViews.Tasks.Partials.Card
```

### Benefits Over Manual Attributes

| Manual (`[SwapViewSource]`) | Auto-Scan |
|-----------------------------|-----------|
| Requires attribute per folder | Zero configuration |
| Must keep in sync | Always current |
| Separate files for views/elements | Single generated file |

---

## VALIDATION

Server-side validation with client-side display.

### View Setup

```html
<form hx-post="/Products/Create" hx-target="#form-container">
    <div class="form-group">
        <label asp-for="Name"></label>
        <input asp-for="Name" class="form-control" />
        <swap-validation for="Name" />
    </div>
    
    <div class="form-group">
        <label asp-for="Price"></label>
        <input asp-for="Price" type="number" class="form-control" />
        <swap-validation for="Price" />
    </div>
    
    <button type="submit">Create</button>
</form>
```

### Controller

```csharp
[HttpPost]
public IActionResult Create(CreateProductDto dto)
{
    if (!ModelState.IsValid)
    {
        return this.SwapValidationErrors(ModelState)
            .WithView("_CreateForm", dto)
            .Build();
    }
    
    var product = _service.Create(dto);
    return this.SwapRedirect("/Products", "Product created!");
}
```

### Validation Methods

```csharp
// Event-based (triggers client-side update)
this.SwapValidationErrors(ModelState)

// OOB-based (server renders each error)
this.SwapValidationErrorsOob(ModelState)

// Add to existing builder
.WithValidationErrors(ModelState)

// Clear specific fields
.ClearValidationErrors(new[] { "Name", "Email" })
```

---

## OUT-OF-BAND SWAPS

Update multiple page elements in a single response.

### Basic Pattern

```csharp
return this.SwapResponse()
    .WithView("_ProductAdded", product)                          // Main target
    .AlsoUpdate("cart-count", "_CartCount", cart.ItemCount)      // OOB 1
    .AlsoUpdate("cart-total", "_CartTotal", cart.Total)          // OOB 2
    .WithSuccessToast("Added to cart!")
    .Build();
```

### HTML Output

```html
<!-- Main content (goes to hx-target) -->
<div class="product-added">Product added!</div>

<!-- OOB swap 1 -->
<div id="cart-count" hx-swap-oob="true">5</div>

<!-- OOB swap 2 -->
<div id="cart-total" hx-swap-oob="true">$99.99</div>
```

### Conditional OOB

```csharp
// Only include if condition is true
.AlsoUpdateIf(user.IsAdmin, "admin-panel", "_AdminPanel", adminData)

// Only swap if element exists in DOM
.AlsoUpdateIfExists("sidebar-stats", "_Stats", stats)
```

### Bulk Updates

```csharp
// Update multiple items
.AlsoUpdateMany(
    items: changedProducts,
    idSelector: p => $"product-{p.Id}",
    viewName: "_ProductRow"
)
```

---

## REALTIME: SERVER-SENT EVENTS (SSE)

Push updates to connected clients.

### Setup

```csharp
// Program.cs
builder.Services.AddSwapHtmx()
    .AddSseEventBridge();

app.UseSwapHtmx()
   .UseSseEventBridge();
```

### Create SSE Endpoint

```csharp
public class DashboardController : SwapController
{
    [HttpGet("/dashboard/stream")]
    public IActionResult DashboardStream()
    {
        return ServerSentEvents(async (conn, ct) =>
        {
            conn.WithEvents("stats-update", "activity-update");
            await conn.KeepAlive(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(30), ct);
        });
    }
}
```

### Client-Side

```html
<div hx-ext="sse" sse-connect="/dashboard/stream">
    <div id="stats" sse-swap="stats-update">
        <!-- Updated via SSE -->
    </div>
</div>
```

### Configure Event Reactions

```csharp
public class DashboardSseConfig : ISwapEventConfiguration
{
    public void Configure(SwapEventBusOptions events)
    {
        events.When(SseEvents.Broadcast("stats-update"))
            .RefreshPartial("stats", "_Stats", ctx =>
            {
                var svc = ctx.RequestServices.GetRequiredService<IStatsService>();
                return svc.GetStats();
            });
    }
}
```

### Broadcast from Controller

```csharp
return this.SwapResponse()
    .WithView("_Task", task)
    .BroadcastSse("stats-update")  // All connected clients receive update
    .Build();
```

---

## REALTIME: WEBSOCKETS

Full-duplex communication.

### Setup

```csharp
// Program.cs
builder.Services.AddSwapHtmx()
    .AddSseEventBridge();

app.UseWebSockets();
app.UseSwapHtmx()
   .UseSseEventBridge();

// Endpoint
app.MapGet("/swap/ws", (IRealtimeConnectionRegistry registry) => 
    SwapResults.WebSocket(registry, options =>
    {
        options.AutoSubscribeRooms = new[] { "global" };
    }));
```

### Client-Side

```html
<div hx-ext="ws" ws-connect="/swap/ws">
    <div id="notifications"></div>
    
    <form ws-send>
        <input type="hidden" name="event" value="chat.message" />
        <input type="text" name="payload" />
        <button type="submit">Send</button>
    </form>
</div>
```

### Rooms

```csharp
// Join room
connection.JoinRoom("room-1");

// Broadcast to room
events.When(RoomEvents.Update)
    .Broadcast("room:room-1")
    .RefreshPartial(...);
```

---

## CONTROLLER PATTERNS

### SwapController (Inheritance)

```csharp
public class ProductsController : SwapController
{
    // SwapView auto-detects HTMX vs full page
    public IActionResult Index()
    {
        return SwapView(products);  // Full page or partial based on HX-Request
    }
    
    // SwapResponse for coordinated updates
    public IActionResult Create(ProductDto dto)
    {
        var product = _service.Create(dto);
        return SwapResponse()
            .WithView("_ProductRow", product)
            .AlsoUpdate("product-count", "_ProductCount", _service.Count())
            .WithCreatedToast("Product", product.Name)
            .Build();
    }
    
    // SwapEvent for event chains
    public IActionResult Delete(int id)
    {
        _service.Delete(id);
        return SwapEvent(ProductEvents.Deleted, new { Id = id });
    }
}
```

### Standard Controller (Composition)

```csharp
using Swap.Htmx;

public class ProductsController : Controller
{
    public IActionResult Index()
    {
        return this.SwapView(products);
    }
    
    public IActionResult Create(ProductDto dto)
    {
        var product = _service.Create(dto);
        return this.SwapResponse()
            .WithView("_ProductRow", product)
            .WithCreatedToast("Product", product.Name)
            .Build();
    }
    
    public IActionResult Delete(int id)
    {
        _service.Delete(id);
        return this.SwapEvent(ProductEvents.Deleted).Build();
    }
}
```

---

## RAZOR PAGES

```csharp
using Swap.Htmx;

public class IndexModel : PageModel
{
    public IActionResult OnPostUpdate()
    {
        return this.SwapResponse()
            .WithView("_Partial", this)
            .Build();
    }
    
    public IActionResult OnPostDelete(int id)
    {
        _service.Delete(id);
        return this.SwapEvent(new ItemDeletedEvent { Id = id }).Build();
    }
}
```

---

## MINIMAL APIs

```csharp
using Swap.Htmx;

// Basic response
app.MapGet("/products", () => 
    SwapResults.Response()
        .WithView("_ProductList", products));

// With OOB and toast
app.MapPost("/products", (ProductDto dto, IProductService service) =>
{
    var product = service.Create(dto);
    return SwapResults.Response()
        .WithView("_ProductRow", product)
        .AlsoUpdate("product-count", "_ProductCount", service.Count())
        .WithCreatedToast("Product");
});

// Trigger event
app.MapDelete("/products/{id}", (int id) =>
    SwapResults.Event(ProductEvents.Deleted, new { Id = id }));
```

---

## CRUD TOAST PRESETS

```csharp
.WithCreatedToast("Product")              // "Product created successfully"
.WithCreatedToast("Product", "Widget")    // "Product 'Widget' created successfully"
.WithUpdatedToast("Settings")             // "Settings updated"
.WithDeletedToast("User", "john@example.com")  // "User 'john@example.com' deleted"
.WithSavedToast("Document")               // "Document saved"
.WithCrudToast(CrudOperation.Archived, "Record")  // "Record archived"
.WithCrudToast(CrudOperation.Restored, "Record")  // "Record restored"
.WithCrudToast(CrudOperation.Duplicated, "Template")  // "Template duplicated"
```

---

## COMMON PATTERNS

### Tab Navigation with State

```csharp
public class TabState : SwapState
{
    public string Tab { get; set; } = "all";
}

public IActionResult ChangeTab([FromQuery] string tab, [FromSwapState] TabState state)
{
    state.Tab = tab;
    return this.SwapResponse()
        .WithView("_TabContent", GetContent(tab))
        .WithState(state)
        .WithTrigger(AppEvents.UI.StateChanged)
        .Build();
}
```

```html
<swap-state state="Model.State" />

<button hx-post="/Items/ChangeTab?tab=all"
        hx-target="#content"
        hx-include="#tab-state">All</button>

<button hx-post="/Items/ChangeTab?tab=active"
        hx-target="#content"
        hx-include="#tab-state">Active</button>
```

### Search + Pagination

```csharp
public class SearchState : SwapState
{
    public string? Query { get; set; }
    public int Page { get; set; } = 1;
}

public IActionResult Search([FromSwapState] SearchState state, string? query)
{
    state.Query = query;
    state.Page = 1;  // Reset on new search
    
    var results = _service.Search(state.Query, state.Page);
    return this.SwapResponse()
        .WithView("_Results", results)
        .AlsoUpdate("pagination", "_Pagination", results.TotalPages)
        .WithState(state)
        .Build();
}
```

### Modal Form

```csharp
// Open modal
public IActionResult EditModal(int id)
{
    var item = _service.Get(id);
    return this.SwapResponse()
        .AlsoUpdate("modal-container", "_EditModal", item)
        .Build();
}

// Save and close
public IActionResult Save(int id, EditDto dto)
{
    if (!ModelState.IsValid)
        return this.SwapValidationErrors(ModelState).WithView("_EditForm", dto).Build();
    
    var item = _service.Update(id, dto);
    return this.SwapResponse()
        .AlsoUpdate($"item-{id}", "_ItemRow", item)
        .AlsoUpdate("modal-container", "_Empty", null)  // Close modal
        .WithUpdatedToast("Item", item.Name)
        .Build();
}
```

### Infinite Scroll

```csharp
public IActionResult LoadMore([FromSwapState] ListState state)
{
    state.Page++;
    var items = _service.GetPage(state.Page);
    
    return this.SwapResponse()
        .AlsoUpdate("item-list", "_Items", items, SwapMode.BeforeEnd)  // Append
        .AlsoUpdateIf(items.HasMore, "load-more", "_LoadMore", state.Page + 1)
        .AlsoUpdateIf(!items.HasMore, "load-more", "_Empty", null, SwapMode.Delete)
        .WithState(state)
        .Build();
}
```

---

## TAG HELPERS REFERENCE

### swap-state

```html
<swap-state state="Model.State" />
<swap-state state="Model.State" id="custom-id" />
<swap-state state="Model.State" prefix="filter" />
<swap-state state="Model.State" include-container="false" />
```

### swap-validation

```html
<swap-validation for="PropertyName" />
<swap-validation for="PropertyName" class="text-danger" />
<swap-validation for="PropertyName" id-prefix="err-" />
```

### swap-include-state

```html
<!-- Auto-includes state container in HTMX requests -->
<button hx-get="/Action" swap-include-state="my-state">Load</button>
```

### swap-hidden

Convenience tag helper for hidden form fields with auto-formatting.

```html
<!-- Basic usage -->
<swap-hidden name="page" value="@Model.Page" />
<swap-hidden name="search" value="@Model.Search" />

<!-- DateTime auto-formats to yyyy-MM-dd -->
<swap-hidden name="startDate" value="@Model.StartDate" />

<!-- Custom date format -->
<swap-hidden name="timestamp" value="@Model.Timestamp" date-format="yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss" />

<!-- Booleans render as lowercase true/false -->
<swap-hidden name="includeArchived" value="@Model.IncludeArchived" />

<!-- Collections render as comma-separated values -->
<swap-hidden name="selectedIds" value="@Model.SelectedIds" />
```

### swap-nav

SPA-style navigation links (see `<swap-nav>` TAG HELPER section above).

```html
<swap-nav to="/products">Products</swap-nav>
<swap-nav to="/orders" target="#sidebar">Orders</swap-nav>
<swap-nav to="/modal" push-url="false">Open Modal</swap-nav>
```

---

## DEBUGGING

Enable debug logging:
```json
// appsettings.Development.json
{
  "Logging": {
    "LogLevel": {
      "Swap.Htmx": "Debug"
    }
  }
}
```

Dev dashboard (if enabled):
```
/_swap/dashboard
```

---

## COMPLEX FRONT-ENDS (50+ Partials)

Swap.Htmx scales to pages with 50-100+ partials through distributed handlers.

### The Problem

A complex page might have dozens of components that need to stay in sync. When a task is completed:

1. The task card moves to "Done" column
2. Stats panel updates (completed count, progress %)
3. Activity feed shows new entry
4. Notification badge updates
5. Progress bar animates
6. Team workload section updates
7. Overdue widget recalculates
8. ... and potentially 10+ more partials

### The Solution

**Controller fires ONE event:**

```csharp
[HttpPost]
public IActionResult CompleteTask(int id)
{
    var task = _tasks.GetById(id);
    _tasks.UpdateStatus(id, TaskStatus.Done);
    
    // Controller doesn't know what updates
    return SwapEvent(new TaskCompletedEvent(task.Id, task.ProjectId, task.Title))
        .WithSuccessToast($"Completed: {task.Title}")
        .Build();
}
```

**Distributed handlers each update ONE partial:**

```csharp
// Handler 1: Stats panel
[SwapHandler]
public class StatsHandler : ISwapEventHandler<TaskCompletedEvent>
{
    public Task HandleAsync(TaskCompletedEvent e, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        var stats = _tasks.GetStats(e.ProjectId);
        builder.AlsoUpdate("stats-panel", "_StatsPanel", stats);
        return Task.CompletedTask;
    }
}

// Handler 2: Kanban columns
[SwapHandler]
public class KanbanHandler : ISwapEventHandler<TaskCompletedEvent>
{
    public Task HandleAsync(TaskCompletedEvent e, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        var kanban = _tasks.GetKanban(e.ProjectId);
        builder.AlsoUpdate("kanban-todo", "_KanbanColumn", kanban.TodoTasks);
        builder.AlsoUpdate("kanban-done", "_KanbanColumn", kanban.DoneTasks);
        return Task.CompletedTask;
    }
}

// Handler 3: Activity feed
[SwapHandler]
public class ActivityHandler : ISwapEventHandler<TaskCompletedEvent>
{
    public Task HandleAsync(TaskCompletedEvent e, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        var activities = _activities.GetRecent(10);
        builder.AlsoUpdate("activity-feed", "_ActivityFeed", activities);
        return Task.CompletedTask;
    }
}

// Handler 4: Progress bar
[SwapHandler]
public class ProgressHandler : ISwapEventHandler<TaskCompletedEvent>
{
    public Task HandleAsync(TaskCompletedEvent e, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        var stats = _tasks.GetStats(e.ProjectId);
        builder.AlsoUpdate("progress-bar", "_ProgressBar", stats);
        return Task.CompletedTask;
    }
}

// ... 6+ more handlers for other partials
```

### How It Works

```
User clicks "Complete" → Controller fires TaskCompletedEvent
        │
        ▼
Event Bus finds all handlers for TaskCompletedEvent
        │
        ├──→ StatsHandler         → AlsoUpdate("stats-panel")
        ├──→ KanbanHandler        → AlsoUpdate("kanban-*")
        ├──→ ActivityHandler      → AlsoUpdate("activity-feed")
        ├──→ ProgressHandler      → AlsoUpdate("progress-bar")
        ├──→ NotificationHandler  → AlsoUpdate("notification-badge")
        └──→ TeamHandler          → AlsoUpdate("team-workload")
        │
        ▼
Response Builder collects all OOB swaps into ONE response
        │
        ▼
Browser (HTMX) swaps each element in place
```

### Why This Scales

| Concern | Without Swap.Htmx | With Swap.Htmx |
|---------|-------------------|----------------|
| Adding new partial | Edit controller + wire up hx-trigger | Add handler class |
| Controller complexity | Knows all 10+ targets | Just fires event |
| Testing | Hard to test partials | Each handler is unit testable |
| Performance | N/A | Only relevant handlers run |

### Conditional Updates

```csharp
// Only update if element exists on page
builder.AlsoUpdateIfExists($"task-detail-{e.TaskId}", "_TaskDetail", task);

// Only update if condition is true
if (_userContext.IsAdmin)
{
    builder.AlsoUpdate("admin-panel", "_AdminPanel", adminData);
}
```

### Bulk Updates

```csharp
// Update many similar elements
builder.AlsoUpdateMany(
    items: affectedProducts,
    idSelector: p => $"product-{p.Id}",
    viewName: "_ProductRow"
);
```

### Organizing Handlers

```
Handlers/
├── Tasks/
│   ├── TaskCardHandler.cs
│   ├── KanbanHandler.cs
│   └── TaskDetailHandler.cs
├── Dashboard/
│   ├── StatsHandler.cs
│   ├── ProgressHandler.cs
│   └── ActivityHandler.cs
└── Notifications/
    └── BadgeHandler.cs
```

**Demo:** See `/demo/SwapDashboard` for a working example with 20+ partials.

---

## PERFORMANCE & SCALING

### Real-World Metrics

**Analytics Dashboard (50+ partials):**
- Single purchase action updates 15-20 components
- Response time: 155ms (including all handler execution and rendering)
- Bundle size: 22KB (HTMX + swap.client.js)
- Zero client-side state management overhead

**Compare to React SPA:**
- Bundle size: 877KB (React + dependencies)
- Initial load: 2.1s
- Runtime memory: 15-25MB (virtual DOM)
- Swap.Htmx: No virtual DOM, no client state sync, no hydration

### Why Distributed Handlers Scale

```
10 partials updated  = 155ms
50 partials updated  = 155ms  (only relevant handlers run)
100 partials updated = ~160ms
```

**Why it's linear:**
- Event bus only invokes handlers registered for that specific event
- Each handler is independent (no locks, no shared state beyond DI)
- Razor rendering is highly optimized (compiled views)
- OOB swaps are batched into single HTTP response

### Optimization Tips

**1. Use AlsoUpdateIf for conditional updates**
```csharp
// ❌ Wasteful - renders partial even when not needed
if (someCondition)
    builder.AlsoUpdate("target", "_View", model);

// ✅ Efficient - checks condition first, skips rendering
builder.AlsoUpdateIf(someCondition, "target", "_View", model);
```

**2. Use AlsoUpdateIfExists for optional elements**
```csharp
// ✅ Only updates if element exists on current page
builder.AlsoUpdateIfExists($"product-detail-{id}", "_ProductDetail", product);
```

**3. Bulk updates for similar elements**
```csharp
// ❌ Slow - individual updates
foreach (var product in affectedProducts)
    builder.AlsoUpdate($"product-{product.Id}", "_ProductCard", product);

// ✅ Fast - single collection pass
builder.AlsoUpdateMany(affectedProducts, p => $"product-{p.Id}", "_ProductCard");
```

**4. Cache expensive queries in handlers**
```csharp
[SwapHandler]
public class StatsHandler : ISwapEventHandler<PurchaseEvent>
{
    private readonly IMemoryCache _cache;
    
    public async Task HandleAsync(PurchaseEvent e, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        var stats = await _cache.GetOrCreateAsync("stats", async entry =>
        {
            entry.SlidingExpiration = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5);
            return await _db.GetStats();
        });
        
        builder.AlsoUpdate("stats", "_Stats", stats);
    }
}
```

**5. Async handlers for I/O-bound work**
```csharp
// ✅ Async for database/API calls
public async Task HandleAsync(ProductEvent e, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
{
    var product = await _db.Products.FindAsync(e.ProductId, ct);
    builder.AlsoUpdate($"product-{e.ProductId}", "_ProductCard", product);
}
```

### Network Performance

**Traditional SPA approach (React/Vue):**
```
1. Click button
2. POST request (50KB bundle already loaded)
3. JSON response (2KB)
4. React re-renders 60 components (only 15 changed)
5. useMemo/React.memo needed to prevent cascade
```

**Swap.Htmx approach:**
```
1. Click button
2. POST request (minimal payload)
3. HTML response (5KB - only 15 partials)
4. HTMX swaps 15 elements (surgical DOM updates)
5. No optimization needed
```

### When NOT to Use Swap.Htmx

**Don't use for:**
- Offline-first apps (service workers, local DB)
- Real-time collaborative editing (Google Docs style)
- Complex client-side state machines (checkout wizards with 10+ steps)
- Heavy client-side computation (image processing, 3D rendering)

**Use React/SPA instead for:**
- Apps that must work offline
- Rich text editors, spreadsheets, CAD tools
- Real-time multiplayer experiences
- Mobile apps (React Native)

**Use Swap.Htmx for:**
- Admin dashboards
- E-commerce sites
- CRM/ERP systems
- Content management
- Analytics dashboards
- Task management
- Most CRUD apps (90% of web apps)

---

## VALIDATION

### Automatic Error Handling

Swap.Htmx automatically renders validation errors next to form fields.

```html
<!-- Form -->
<form hx-post="/Products/Create" hx-target="#form-container">
    <div>
        <label asp-for="Name"></label>
        <input asp-for="Name" />
        <span asp-validation-for="Name"></span>
    </div>
    
    <div>
        <label asp-for="Price"></label>
        <input asp-for="Price" />
        <span asp-validation-for="Price"></span>
    </div>
    
    <button type="submit">Create</button>
</form>
```

```csharp
// Controller
[HttpPost]
public IActionResult Create(ProductDto dto)
{
    if (!ModelState.IsValid)
    {
        // Returns form with validation errors highlighted
        return this.SwapValidationErrors(ModelState)
            .WithView("_ProductForm", dto)
            .Build();
    }
    
    var product = _service.Create(dto);
    
    return this.SwapResponse()
        .WithView("_ProductCard", product)
        .WithCreatedToast("Product", product.Name)
        .Build();
}
```

### Custom Validation Messages

```csharp
if (!_service.IsUnique(dto.Sku))
{
    ModelState.AddModelError(nameof(dto.Sku), "SKU must be unique");
    return this.SwapValidationErrors(ModelState)
        .WithView("_ProductForm", dto)
        .Build();
}
```

### HTMX Validation Extension

For client-side validation before submit:

```html
<form hx-post="/Products/Create" 
      hx-ext="validation"
      hx-target="#form-container">
    
    <input name="email" 
           type="email" 
           required 
           pattern="[a-z0-9._%+-]+@[a-z0-9.-]+\.[a-z]{2,}$" />
    
    <button type="submit">Submit</button>
</form>
```

### Full-Page vs Partial Validation

```csharp
// For modal forms (swap form only)
if (!ModelState.IsValid)
    return this.SwapValidationErrors(ModelState)
        .WithView("_ModalForm", dto)
        .Build();

// For full-page forms (redirect on success)
if (!ModelState.IsValid)
    return View("Create", dto);  // Full page with layout

_service.Create(dto);
return this.SwapResponse()
    .WithNavigation("/products")
    .WithCreatedToast("Product")
    .Build();
```

---

## ANTI-PATTERNS TO AVOID

### ❌ Anti-Pattern 1: Controller Knows All UI Targets

```csharp
// ❌ BAD - Tightly coupled, controller knows entire UI structure
[HttpPost]
public IActionResult Purchase(int productId)
{
    _cart.AddItem(productId);
    
    return this.SwapResponse()
        .AlsoUpdate("cart-count", "_CartCount", _cart.ItemCount)
        .AlsoUpdate("cart-total", "_CartTotal", _cart.Total)
        .AlsoUpdate("cart-items", "_CartItems", _cart.Items)
        .AlsoUpdate($"product-{productId}", "_ProductCard", _service.GetById(productId))
        .AlsoUpdate("header-cart", "_HeaderCart", _cart)
        .AlsoUpdate("recommendations", "_Recommendations", _recommendations.GetForCart(_cart))
        .WithSuccessToast("Added to cart")
        .Build();
}
```

**Problem:** Controller is bloated, hard to test, difficult to extend.

```csharp
// ✅ GOOD - Fire event, let handlers decide what updates
[HttpPost]
public IActionResult Purchase(int productId)
{
    _cart.AddItem(productId);
    
    return SwapEvent(new CartItemAddedEvent { ProductId = productId })
        .WithSuccessToast("Added to cart")
        .Build();
}

// Handlers are small, focused, independently testable
[SwapHandler]
public class CartCountHandler : ISwapEventHandler<CartItemAddedEvent>
{
    public Task HandleAsync(CartItemAddedEvent e, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        builder.AlsoUpdate("cart-count", "_CartCount", _cart.ItemCount);
        return Task.CompletedTask;
    }
}
```

### ❌ Anti-Pattern 2: Using hx-trigger for Multi-Component Updates

```html
<!-- ❌ BAD - Brittle, verbose, difficult to maintain -->
<button hx-post="/Cart/AddItem"
        hx-swap="none"
        _="on htmx:afterRequest
           call htmx.ajax('GET', '/Cart/Count', {target: '#cart-count'})
           call htmx.ajax('GET', '/Cart/Total', {target: '#cart-total'})
           call htmx.ajax('GET', '/Cart/Items', {target: '#cart-items'})">
    Add to Cart
</button>
```

**Problem:** Multiple HTTP requests (N+1), client-side logic, fragile.

```html
<!-- ✅ GOOD - One request, server coordinates via OOB swaps -->
<button hx-post="/Cart/AddItem" hx-swap="none">
    Add to Cart
</button>
```

```csharp
// Server handles coordination in one response
return SwapEvent(new CartItemAddedEvent { ProductId = productId }).Build();
```

### ❌ Anti-Pattern 3: Client-Side State Management

```javascript
// ❌ BAD - Duplicating state on client
let currentTab = 'overview';
document.querySelectorAll('.tab-btn').forEach(btn => {
    btn.addEventListener('click', () => {
        currentTab = btn.dataset.tab;
        htmx.ajax('GET', `/Dashboard/Tab?tab=${currentTab}`, '#content');
    });
});
```

**Problem:** State drift, synchronization bugs, complex client code.

```csharp
// ✅ GOOD - SwapState manages state on server
public class DashboardState : SwapState
{
    public string Tab { get; set; } = "overview";
}

public IActionResult Tab([FromSwapState] DashboardState state, string tab)
{
    state.Tab = tab;  // Server is source of truth
    return this.SwapResponse()
        .WithView($"_Tab{tab}", data)
        .WithState(state)  // Syncs back to client
        .Build();
}
```

### ❌ Anti-Pattern 4: Manual ID Management

```csharp
// ❌ BAD - Magic strings, typos, hard to refactor
builder.AlsoUpdate("prodcut-list", "_ProductList", products);  // Typo!
builder.AlsoUpdate("product-count", "_ProductCoutn", count);   // Typo!
```

```csharp
// ✅ GOOD - Generated constants
[SwapElementSource("Views/Products")]
public static partial class ProductElements { }

builder.AlsoUpdate(ProductElements.ProductList, ProductViews.Partials.ProductList, products);
```

### ❌ Anti-Pattern 5: Ignoring hx-swap-oob

```html
<!-- ❌ BAD - Manually triggering updates via JavaScript -->
<button onclick="updateAllComponents()">Purchase</button>

<script>
function updateAllComponents() {
    fetch('/Cart/Purchase', { method: 'POST' })
        .then(() => {
            htmx.ajax('GET', '/Cart/Count', '#cart-count');
            htmx.ajax('GET', '/Cart/Total', '#cart-total');
            // ... more manual requests
        });
}
</script>
```

```html
<!-- ✅ GOOD - Let HTMX handle OOB swaps automatically -->
<button hx-post="/Cart/Purchase" hx-swap="none">Purchase</button>
```

## COMPLEX FRONT-ENDS (50+ Partials)

Swap.Htmx scales to pages with 50-100+ partials through distributed handlers.

### Real-World Example: Analytics Dashboard

**Location:** `/demo/SwapSmallPartials/src/Modules/Analytics/`

This is a complete working example showing:
- **50+ partials:** 6 KPIs, 12 products, 6 categories, 8 regions, 24 hourly bars, activity feed
- **One button click:** Updates 15-20 components via single HTTP request
- **155ms response time:** Including all handler execution, rendering, and OOB swaps
- **650 lines of code:** Compare to equivalent React SPA (1,200 lines)

### The Architecture

**1. Controller fires ONE event:**

```csharp
[HttpPost]
public IActionResult SimulatePurchase()
{
    // Update state (single source of truth)
    var productId = _state.Products[Random.Shared.Next(_state.Products.Count)].Id;
    var region = _regions[Random.Shared.Next(_regions.Length)];
    _state.ProcessPurchase(productId, region, customer: "John D.");
    
    // Fire event - controller doesn't know what updates
    return SwapEvent(AnalyticsEvents.Purchase.Completed, new PurchaseCompletedEvent
    {
        ProductId = productId,
        Region = region,
        CustomerName = "John D."
    }).Build();
}
```

**2. Distributed handlers (15 handlers across 8 files):**

```csharp
// Handlers/KpiHandlers.cs - Updates all 6 KPI cards
[SwapHandler]
public class RevenueHandler : ISwapEventHandler<PurchaseCompletedEvent>
{
    private readonly AnalyticsState _state;
    public RevenueHandler(AnalyticsState state) => _state = state;
    
    public Task HandleAsync(PurchaseCompletedEvent e, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        builder.AlsoUpdate("revenue-today", "_RevenueToday", _state);
        return Task.CompletedTask;
    }
}

[SwapHandler]
public class OrdersHandler : ISwapEventHandler<PurchaseCompletedEvent>
{
    private readonly AnalyticsState _state;
    public OrdersHandler(AnalyticsState state) => _state = state;
    
    public Task HandleAsync(PurchaseCompletedEvent e, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        builder.AlsoUpdate("orders-count", "_OrdersCount", _state);
        return Task.CompletedTask;
    }
}

// Handlers/ProductHandlers.cs - Updates 12 product cards
[SwapHandler]
public class ProductCardHandler : ISwapEventHandler<PurchaseCompletedEvent>
{
    private readonly AnalyticsState _state;
    public ProductCardHandler(AnalyticsState state) => _state = state;
    
    public Task HandleAsync(PurchaseCompletedEvent e, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        // Only update the specific product that was purchased
        var product = _state.Products.First(p => p.Id == e.ProductId);
        builder.AlsoUpdate($"product-{e.ProductId}", "_ProductCard", product);
        return Task.CompletedTask;
    }
}

// Handlers/RegionHandlers.cs - Updates 8 region cards
[SwapHandler]
public class RegionCardHandler : ISwapEventHandler<PurchaseCompletedEvent>
{
    private readonly AnalyticsState _state;
    public RegionCardHandler(AnalyticsState state) => _state = state;
    
    public Task HandleAsync(PurchaseCompletedEvent e, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        // Only update the specific region
        var region = _state.Regions.First(r => r.Name == e.Region);
        builder.AlsoUpdate($"region-{e.Region}", "_RegionCard", region);
        return Task.CompletedTask;
    }
}

// Handlers/ActivityHandlers.cs - Updates activity feed
[SwapHandler]
public class ActivityFeedHandler : ISwapEventHandler<PurchaseCompletedEvent>
{
    private readonly AnalyticsState _state;
    public ActivityFeedHandler(AnalyticsState state) => _state = state;
    
    public Task HandleAsync(PurchaseCompletedEvent e, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        builder.AlsoUpdate("activity-feed", "_ActivityFeed", _state);
        return Task.CompletedTask;
    }
}

// ... 11 more handlers (categories, hourly data, customer metrics, etc.)
```

**3. Server returns ONE HTTP response with 15 OOB swaps:**

```html
<!-- Main response (toast) -->
<div class="toast success">Purchase completed!</div>

<!-- OOB Swap 1: Revenue KPI -->
<div id="revenue-today" hx-swap-oob="true">
    <div class="kpi-card">
        <h3>Revenue Today</h3>
        <p class="kpi-value">$4,523.75</p>
        <span class="kpi-change positive">+12.5%</span>
    </div>
</div>

<!-- OOB Swap 2: Orders KPI -->
<div id="orders-count" hx-swap-oob="true">
    <div class="kpi-card">
        <h3>Orders</h3>
        <p class="kpi-value">127</p>
        <span class="kpi-change positive">+8</span>
    </div>
</div>

<!-- OOB Swap 3: Specific product card -->
<div id="product-5" hx-swap-oob="true">
    <div class="product-card">
        <h4>Wireless Mouse</h4>
        <p class="sales">Sales: $234.50</p>
        <span class="stock text-danger">8 left</span>
    </div>
</div>

<!-- ... 12 more OOB swaps (other KPIs, categories, regions, hourly bars, activity) -->
```

**4. HTMX swaps each element in 155ms:**

```
User clicks "Purchase" → HTTP POST → Server processes event → 15 handlers run in parallel
→ Response built with 15 OOB swaps → Browser receives → HTMX swaps each element → Done
```

### Why This Scales

| Challenge | Without Events | With Events |
|-----------|----------------|-------------|
| **Adding new partial** | Edit controller, add AlsoUpdate call | Create new handler class |
| **Controller complexity** | Knows all 15+ UI targets | Just fires event |
| **Testing** | Hard to test partial updates | Each handler is unit testable |
| **Performance** | N/A | Only relevant handlers run |
| **Maintainability** | Brittle, tightly coupled | Decoupled, SOLID principles |

### The Problem with Traditional Approach

A complex dashboard might have:
- Task card moves to "Done" column
- Stats panel updates (completed count, progress %)
- Activity feed shows new entry
- Notification badge updates
- Progress bar animates
- Team workload section updates
- Overdue widget recalculates
- ... and potentially 10+ more partials

**Without event-driven architecture:**
```csharp
// ❌ Controller becomes a 100+ line mess
[HttpPost]
public IActionResult CompleteTask(int id)
{
