```csharp
    _tasks.UpdateStatus(id, TaskStatus.Done);
    
    return this.SwapResponse()
        .AlsoUpdate("task-row-" + id, "_TaskRow", _tasks.GetById(id))
        .AlsoUpdate("stats-panel", "_StatsPanel", _tasks.GetStats())
        .AlsoUpdate("kanban-todo", "_KanbanColumn", _tasks.GetByStatus(TaskStatus.Todo))
        .AlsoUpdate("kanban-done", "_KanbanColumn", _tasks.GetByStatus(TaskStatus.Done))
        .AlsoUpdate("activity-feed", "_ActivityFeed", _activities.GetRecent(10))
        .AlsoUpdate("progress-bar", "_ProgressBar", _tasks.GetProgress())
        .AlsoUpdate("notification-badge", "_NotificationBadge", _notifications.Count())
        .AlsoUpdate("team-workload", "_TeamWorkload", _teams.GetWorkload())
        .AlsoUpdate("overdue-widget", "_OverdueWidget", _tasks.GetOverdue())
        // ... 5+ more updates
        .Build();
}
```

**Problem:** Unmaintainable, untestable, violates Single Responsibility Principle.

**With event-driven architecture:**
```csharp
// ✅ Controller is clean, focused
[HttpPost]
public IActionResult CompleteTask(int id)
{
    var task = _tasks.GetById(id);
    _tasks.UpdateStatus(id, TaskStatus.Done);
    
    return SwapEvent(new TaskCompletedEvent(task.Id, task.ProjectId, task.Title))
        .WithSuccessToast($"Completed: {task.Title}")
        .Build();
}

// Each handler is small, focused, testable
[SwapHandler]
public class StatsHandler : ISwapEventHandler<TaskCompletedEvent>
{
    public Task HandleAsync(TaskCompletedEvent e, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        var stats = _tasks.GetStats(e.ProjectId);
        builder.AlsoUpdate("stats-panel", "_StatsPanel", stats);
        return Task.CompletedTask;
    }
}
```

### Handler Organization

```
Modules/Analytics/Events/Handlers/
├── KpiHandlers.cs           (6 handlers for KPI cards)
├── ProductHandlers.cs       (12 handlers for product cards)
├── CategoryHandlers.cs      (6 handlers for category cards)
├── RegionHandlers.cs        (8 handlers for region cards)
├── HourlyHandlers.cs        (24 handlers for hourly bars)
├── ActivityHandlers.cs      (1 handler for activity feed)
├── CustomerHandlers.cs      (1 handler for customer metrics)
└── ChartHandlers.cs         (3 handlers for charts)
```

**Total:** 61 handlers across 8 files, all auto-discovered via `[SwapHandler]`.

### Conditional & Bulk Updates

```csharp
// Only update if element exists on current page
builder.AlsoUpdateIfExists($"task-detail-{e.TaskId}", "_TaskDetail", task);

// Only update if condition is true
builder.AlsoUpdateIf(_userContext.IsAdmin, "admin-panel", "_AdminPanel", adminData);

// Bulk updates for similar elements
builder.AlsoUpdateMany(
    items: affectedProducts,
    idSelector: p => $"product-{p.Id}",
    viewName: "_ProductRow"
);
```

### Performance Comparison

| Framework | Bundle Size | Response Time | Code (LOC) | HTTP Requests |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|------------|---------------|
| **Swap.Htmx** | 22KB | 155ms | 650 | 1 |
| **React SPA** | 877KB | ~300ms | 1,200 | 1 (but after 2.1s initial load) |
| **Vue SPA** | 650KB | ~250ms | 1,100 | 1 |
| **Traditional MPA** | 0KB | 200ms | 800 | 15 (one per partial) |

**Why Swap.Htmx wins:**
- No client-side framework overhead
- Server-side rendering (no hydration)
- Surgical DOM updates (no virtual DOM diffing)
- One HTTP request with batched OOB swaps
- No client-side state synchronization

### Try It Yourself

```bash
cd demo/SwapSmallPartials
dotnet run

# Navigate to: http://localhost:5000/Analytics
# Click "Simulate Purchase" button
# Watch 15+ components update instantly
# Open DevTools Network tab: See single 155ms request
```

---

## DECISION TREES

### When to Use Distributed Handlers vs Centralized Config

**Use Distributed Handlers ([SwapHandler]) when:**
- ✅ Complex domain with many loosely-coupled concerns
- ✅ Multiple teams/modules contributing handlers
- ✅ Handlers have different dependencies (services, repositories)
- ✅ You want handlers to be independently testable
- ✅ Handlers might have async I/O (database queries, API calls)
- ✅ You need to add/remove handlers without touching controller

**Example:** E-commerce purchase → inventory, analytics, notifications, recommendations all need updates

```csharp
// Good fit for distributed handlers
return SwapEvent(new PurchaseCompletedEvent { ... }).Build();

[SwapHandler] // Inventory module
public class StockHandler : ISwapEventHandler<PurchaseCompletedEvent> { }

[SwapHandler] // Analytics module
public class RevenueHandler : ISwapEventHandler<PurchaseCompletedEvent> { }

[SwapHandler] // Notifications module
public class EmailHandler : ISwapEventHandler<PurchaseCompletedEvent> { }
```

**Use Centralized Config (ISwapEventConfiguration) when:**
- ✅ Simple, declarative UI updates (no complex logic)
- ✅ All updates are straightforward partial refreshes
- ✅ You want to see all event reactions in one place
- ✅ No async I/O needed (just rendering different views)
- ✅ Small team, simple domain

**Example:** Filter button → refresh grid and count

```csharp
// Good fit for centralized config
public class FilterEventConfig : ISwapEventConfiguration
{
    public void Configure(SwapEventBusOptions events)
    {
        events.When(FilterEvents.Applied)
            .RefreshPartial("product-grid", "_ProductGrid", ctx => 
                ctx.RequestServices.GetService<IProductService>().GetFiltered())
            .RefreshPartial("product-count", "_ProductCount", ctx => 
                new { Count = ctx.RequestServices.GetService<IProductService>().Count() })
            .SuccessToast("Filter applied!");
    }
}
```

### When to Use SwapState vs Query Parameters

**Use SwapState when:**
- ✅ State has multiple properties (tab, page, sortBy, search, filters)
- ✅ State changes frequently (every user interaction)
- ✅ You want automatic change tracking
- ✅ State needs validation or business logic
- ✅ You need type-safety

**Example:** Complex dashboard with tabs, pagination, sorting, filters

```csharp
public class DashboardState : SwapState
{
    public string Tab { get; set; } = "overview";
    public int Page { get; set; } = 1;
    public string Search { get; set; } = "";
    public string SortBy { get; set; } = "name";
    public bool SortDesc { get; set; } = false;
    
    // Validation
    public override void OnValidate(ValidationContext context)
    {
        if (Page < 1) Page = 1;
    }
}
```

**Use Query Parameters when:**
- ✅ Single value or simple state (just page number, just search term)
- ✅ State needs to be bookmarkable/shareable
- ✅ SEO matters (search engines need to index different states)
- ✅ External links need to deep-link to specific state

**Example:** Public product listing with pagination

```csharp
public IActionResult List(int page = 1, string? search = null)
{
    var products = _service.GetPage(page, search);
    return this.SwapResponse()
        .WithView("_ProductGrid", products)
        .Build();
}
```

### When to Use Events vs Direct AlsoUpdate

**Use Events (SwapEvent) when:**
- ✅ Multiple components need to update (3+)
- ✅ Updates are cross-cutting concerns (analytics, logging, notifications)
- ✅ You want to decouple controller from UI structure
- ✅ Other modules/teams might want to react to the event
- ✅ You might add more reactions in the future

**Example:** User completes task → many parts of UI update

```csharp
// ✅ Event-driven - extensible, decoupled
return SwapEvent(new TaskCompletedEvent { TaskId = id }).Build();
```

**Use Direct AlsoUpdate when:**
- ✅ Single, obvious UI update (1-2 targets)
- ✅ Tightly coupled to the action (not cross-cutting)
- ✅ Won't change or expand
- ✅ No other modules care about this

**Example:** Toggle favorite → just update the button

```csharp
// ✅ Direct update - simple, obvious
[HttpPost]
public IActionResult ToggleFavorite(int id)
{
    var isFavorite = _service.ToggleFavorite(id);
    return this.SwapResponse()
        .WithView("_FavoriteButton", new { Id = id, IsFavorite = isFavorite })
        .Build();
}
```

### When to Use WithNavigation vs Redirect

**Use WithNavigation when:**
- ✅ HTMX request (SPA-style navigation)
- ✅ You want to preserve toast messages
- ✅ You want to preserve triggers
- ✅ Target element should update (not full page refresh)
- ✅ Want smooth transitions

**Example:** Form submit → navigate to detail page with toast

```csharp
// ✅ WithNavigation - preserves toast, smooth transition
return this.SwapResponse()
    .WithNavigation($"/products/{product.Id}")
    .WithCreatedToast("Product", product.Name)
    .Build();
```

**Use Redirect when:**
- ✅ Full page refresh needed (POST-Redirect-GET pattern)
- ✅ Non-HTMX request (direct browser navigation)
- ✅ External URL or different domain
- ✅ Authentication flow (login → redirect to return URL)

**Example:** Login success → redirect to return URL

```csharp
// ✅ Redirect - standard POST-Redirect-GET
return RedirectToAction("Index", "Dashboard");
```

### Response Pattern Flowchart

```
User action triggers request
    │
    ├─→ Is this an HTMX request? (Request.IsHtmx())
    │   │
    │   ├─→ NO → Use standard MVC
    │   │         return View(model);
    │   │         return RedirectToAction(...);
    │   │
    │   └─→ YES → Does it update multiple components?
    │             │
    │             ├─→ NO (1-2 targets) → Use SwapResponse with AlsoUpdate
    │             │                       return this.SwapResponse()
    │             │                           .WithView("_Main", model)
    │             │                           .AlsoUpdate("count", "_Count", count)
    │             │                           .Build();
    │             │
    │             └─→ YES (3+ targets) → Use SwapEvent with distributed handlers
    │                                     return SwapEvent(new MyEvent { ... }).Build();
    │
    └─→ Does it navigate to different page?
        │
        ├─→ YES → Use WithNavigation (for HTMX) or Redirect (for browser)
        │
        └─→ NO → Use SwapResponse with WithView
```

---

---

## QUICK REFERENCE CARD

### Most Common Patterns (Copy-Paste Ready)

**Simple partial update:**
```csharp
return this.SwapResponse()
    .WithView("_ProductCard", product)
    .Build();
```

**Update multiple partials (3+ targets):**
```csharp
return this.SwapEvent(new ProductUpdatedEvent { ProductId = id })
    .WithSuccessToast("Updated!")
    .Build();
```

**Form with validation:**
```csharp
if (!ModelState.IsValid)
    return this.SwapValidationErrors(ModelState).WithView("_Form", dto).Build();

return this.SwapResponse()
    .WithNavigation("/products")
    .WithCreatedToast("Product", dto.Name)
    .Build();
```

**Delete with confirmation:**
```html
<button hx-delete="/Products/@Model.Id"
        hx-confirm="Delete this item?"
        hx-target="#product-@Model.Id"
        hx-swap="outerHTML swap:1s">
    Delete
</button>
```

```csharp
[HttpDelete]
public IActionResult Delete(int id)
{
    _service.Delete(id);
    return this.SwapResponse()
        .WithView("_Empty", null)  // Removes element
        .WithDeletedToast("Product")
        .Build();
}
```

**Search with debounce:**
```html
<input hx-get="/Products/Search"
       hx-target="#results"
       hx-trigger="keyup changed delay:300ms"
       name="search" />
```

**Modal form:**
```html
<button hx-get="/Products/Edit/@Model.Id"
        hx-target="#modal">
    Edit
</button>
```

**State-based tabs:**
```csharp
public IActionResult Tab([FromSwapState] DashboardState state, string tab)
{
    state.Tab = tab;
    return this.SwapResponse()
        .WithView($"_Tab{tab}", data)
        .WithState(state)
        .Build();
}
```

### Response Builder Cheat Sheet

| Method | Use When | Example |
|--------|----------|---------|
| `.WithView(view, model)` | Main target content | `.WithView("_List", products)` |
| `.AlsoUpdate(id, view, model)` | Update one OOB element | `.AlsoUpdate("count", "_Count", 42)` |
| `.AlsoUpdateMany(items, idFunc, view)` | Update multiple similar elements | `.AlsoUpdateMany(products, p => $"prod-{p.Id}", "_Card")` |
| `.AlsoUpdateIf(condition, ...)` | Conditional update | `.AlsoUpdateIf(isAdmin, "panel", "_AdminPanel", data)` |
| `.AlsoUpdateIfExists(id, ...)` | Update if element on page | `.AlsoUpdateIfExists($"detail-{id}", "_Detail", item)` |
| `.WithNavigation(path)` | SPA-style navigation | `.WithNavigation("/products")` |
| `.WithSuccessToast(msg)` | Success feedback | `.WithSuccessToast("Saved!")` |
| `.WithCreatedToast(entity, name)` | CRUD create feedback | `.WithCreatedToast("Product", "Widget")` |
| `.WithState(state)` | Sync server state to client | `.WithState(filterState)` |
| `.WithTrigger(eventKey)` | Fire client-side event | `.WithTrigger(Events.Updated)` |
| `.WithValidationErrors(modelState)` | Show validation errors | `.WithValidationErrors(ModelState)` |

### HTMX Attributes Cheat Sheet

| Attribute | Purpose | Example |
|-----------|---------|---------|
| `hx-get="/url"` | GET request | `hx-get="/products/list"` |
| `hx-post="/url"` | POST request | `hx-post="/products/create"` |
| `hx-delete="/url"` | DELETE request | `hx-delete="/products/5"` |
| `hx-target="#id"` | Where to swap content | `hx-target="#product-list"` |
| `hx-swap="innerHTML"` | How to swap (innerHTML, outerHTML, etc.) | `hx-swap="outerHTML"` |
| `hx-trigger="event"` | What triggers request | `hx-trigger="click, keyup delay:500ms"` |
| `hx-include="#id"` | Include form data from other element | `hx-include="#filter-form"` |
| `hx-confirm="msg"` | Confirmation dialog | `hx-confirm="Are you sure?"` |
| `hx-indicator="#id"` | Show loading spinner | `hx-indicator="#spinner"` |
| `hx-disabled-elt="this"` | Disable during request | `hx-disabled-elt="this"` |
| `hx-swap-oob="true"` | Out-of-band swap (server-side only) | Server renders this |

### Swap.Htmx Tag Helpers

| Tag Helper | Purpose | Example |
|------------|---------|---------|
| `<swap-state state="Model.State" />` | Render state as hidden inputs | Renders `<div id="filter-state">...</div>` |
| `<swap-nav to="/path">Link</swap-nav>` | SPA-style navigation link | `<swap-nav to="/products">Products</swap-nav>` |

### SwapMode Enum

| Mode | Effect | Use For |
|------|--------|---------|
| `OuterHTML` | Replace entire element (default) | Replacing cards, rows |
| `InnerHTML` | Replace content only | Updating table body |
| `BeforeEnd` | Append as last child | Infinite scroll, adding list items |
| `AfterBegin` | Prepend as first child | Newest-first lists |
| `Delete` | Remove element | Deleting rows, hiding elements |

### Event Handler Template

```csharp
[SwapHandler]
public class MyHandler : ISwapEventHandler<MyEvent>
{
    private readonly IMyService _service;
    
    public MyHandler(IMyService service) => _service = service;
    
    public async Task HandleAsync(MyEvent e, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        var data = await _service.GetDataAsync(e.SomeId, ct);
        builder.AlsoUpdate("target-id", "_PartialView", data);
    }
}
```

### State Class Template

```csharp
public class MyState : SwapState
{
    public string Filter { get; set; } = "";
    public int Page { get; set; } = 1;
    public string SortBy { get; set; } = "name";
    
    // Optional: Add validation
    public override void OnValidate(ValidationContext context)
    {
        if (Page < 1) Page = 1;
    }
}
```

### Troubleshooting Quick Fixes

| Problem | Solution |
|---------|----------|
| Full page returned instead of partial | Enable `AutoSuppressLayout` or add `_ViewStart.cshtml` |
| Toast not showing | Add `<link rel="stylesheet" href="~/_content/Swap.Htmx/css/swap.css" />` |
| Validation errors not appearing | Use `this.SwapValidationErrors(ModelState)` |
| Handlers not firing | Add `[SwapHandler]` attribute to class |
| State not persisting | Add `hx-include="#state-id"` to HTMX element |
| OOB swaps not working | Ensure element IDs match between response and page |

---

## VERSION

This document describes Swap.Htmx v1.0.2 for .NET 9.0.

**Last Updated:** Based on Analytics module implementation (50+ partials, 155ms response time)
